cases.title: Sartorius v. Siouxlands Presbytery
cases.synopsis: TE Sartorius complained that Siouxlands Presbytery erred in acquitting TE Greg Lawrence on five charges of holding Federal Vision and non-confessional views on baptism, perseverance, and covenant of works at a second trial (2015); the SJC denied the complaint 22-1, holding that the complainant failed to demonstrate clear error in the trial court's factual finding and that BCO 39-3.2 requires great deference to the lower court's verdict. A concurring opinion by RE Donahoe extensively discussed standards of appellate review, defendant testimony supporting the acquittal, and raised concerns about due process rights of acquitted persons.

cases.title: Webster & Fowler v. Heritage Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: RE Morris Webster and RE Wayne Fowler

cases.synopsis: RE Webster and RE Fowler complained that Heritage Presbytery exceeded its authority when it passed a motion advising the Session of New Covenant Presbyterian Church to postpone ordaining a candidate who might be found unqualified due to non-subscription to infant baptism; the SJC denied the complaint 20-0, finding that Presbytery merely offered advice that Session was free to reject and did not impinge on Session's constitutional authority.
